content.text:
<user_query>
@solar/Scoring I have a React implementation using Material UI for a set of UI components that implement scoring list items. These list items (or scoring items) work together to distribute a fixed amount of scoring potential across different items.

Please look at the stories and documentation for these scoring components, and port the UI components to ShaDcn-based items. Use the ShaDcn item component as the underlying primitive. Make sure to set up proper spacing and add stories for the scoring component.

Please scaffold these components in the UI lib package. @portugal/packages/ui-lib/src 
</user_query>

Exploring the solar scoring components and the portugal ui-lib package structure to port them to Shadcn-based items.

[REDACTED]

Implementing scoring components: Solar uses MUI `ListItem`-style rows with +/- controls, an avatar score, text, and discrete progress (switching to linear when `max >= 21`). Building Shadcn `Item`-based equivalents in `ui-lib`.

[REDACTED]

Fixing imports (`cn` from `@portugal/ui`), improving `Progress` secondary styling, and adding stories.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Build Storybook for ui-lib to verify stories compile

content.text:
Here’s what was added after reviewing `solar/Scoring` (`ScoreItem`, `ScoringListItem`, `ScoringRemainder`, `DiscreteProgress`, `Complete` + `Scoring.stories.jsx`).

### Location
`packages/ui-lib/src/components/scoring/`

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `ScoringDiscreteProgress.tsx` | Segment bar for small `max`, Radix `Progress` when `max >= linearFrom` (default **21**, same idea as solar). |
| `ScoreItem.tsx` | `ItemMedia` (circular score) + `ItemTitle` / `ItemDescription` + progress; optional `children` instead of labels. |
| `ScoringListItem.tsx` | One row: `Item` + ghost icon `Button`s + `ScoreItem`. |
| `ScoringRemainder.tsx` | Footer-style `Item` (`variant="muted"`, `shadow-md`) wrapping `ScoreItem`; supports `invert` like solar. |
| `ScoringComplete.tsx` | Scrollable `ItemGroup` + rows + remainder; fixed pool + per-row min/max (same rules as `Complete.jsx`). |
| `index.ts` | Public exports. |
| `Scoring.stories.tsx` | **ui-lib/Scoring** docs blurb + stories: full pool, single row, remainder, both progress modes, `ScoreItem` inside `Item`. |

### Primitives (from `@portugal/ui`)
- **Item**, **ItemMedia**, **ItemContent**, **ItemTitle**, **ItemDescription**, **ItemActions**, **ItemGroup**
- **Button**, **ScrollArea**, **Progress**
- **lucide-react** `CircleMinus` / `CirclePlus` instead of MUI icons

Spacing uses your design tokens (`gap-component`, `space-y-section`, `mt-section`, `py-component`, etc.) and list rows use `Item` + horizontal borders so rows read as a single list.

### Package exports
Everything is re-exported from `packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts` (`ScoreItem`, `ScoringListItem`, `ScoringRemainder`, `ScoringDiscreteProgress`, `ScoringComplete`, and their prop types).

`bun run build` in `ui-lib` still fails on **existing** `Card`/`Input` `variant` typing issues elsewhere; `oxlint` on `src/components/scoring` is clean and **`bun run build-storybook`** succeeds (including `Scoring.stories`).

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Since we have now implemented the full scoring UI components, please use these UI components to implement the profile edit panel and distribute the total amount of scoring points over all of the different scoring criteria.

Please group the scoring criteria by category, but allow the user to distribute points across categories however they want.@portugal/apps/frontend-web/src/panels/profile-edit-panel.tsx 
</user_query>

Exploring the profile edit panel and how scoring criteria are structured so we can integrate the scoring UI.

[REDACTED]

Implementing the profile edit panel: using a fixed MARBLE_TARGET pool (same as save normalization), grouping criteria with `groupCriteriaByCategory`, and composing `ScoringListItem` + `ScoringRemainder`. Initializing drafts with `normalizeWeightsToSum` so the session starts as a full distribution.

[REDACTED]

Removing the rounded top on the first row of each category so it sits flush under the category header.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what changed in `profile-edit-panel.tsx`:

### Behavior
- **Fixed pool:** `MARBLE_TARGET` (117) marbles shared across **all** criteria. `+` is enabled only when `pointsLeft > 0`; `-` when the row is > 0. Pool can move **across categories** with no per-category caps.
- **Load:** Draft weights are initialized with `normalizeWeightsToSum(..., MARBLE_TARGET)` so the panel opens as a valid full distribution (same total as save semantics).
- **Save:** Draft is normalized again to 117 for this profile, then `normalizeAllProfileWeightsToMarbleTarget` runs on the full config (unchanged contract).

### UI
- **`groupCriteriaByCategory`** from `scoring-config-mutations.ts` — one bordered block per category (title = category name or id for orphans).
- **`ScoringListItem`** per criterion (name + optional description, marble count, discrete/linear progress vs `MARBLE_TARGET`).
- **`ScoringRemainder`** under the scroll area — “Marbles remaining” / `pointsLeft`.
- **`ScrollArea`** with `h-[min(420px,55vh)]` so long criterion lists stay usable in the panel.

### Copy
Short note that categories are only for grouping and that save still rescales every profile to 117 marbles.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Please don't use internal scrolling; use the scrolling of the edit panel or the cover view scrolling.

Dock the remainder on the bottom, fixed.

</user_query>

Inspecting panel chrome and layout so we can remove inner scrolling and fix the remainder to the bottom.

[REDACTED]

Removing the panel's `ScrollArea`. Using CoverView's built-in scroll and a `sticky bottom-0` bar so the remainder stays docked to the bottom of the panel's scroll viewport.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what changed:

### 1. `CoverView` (`packages/ui/src/components/sewdn/CoverView/CoverView.tsx`)
- New optional prop **`bottomBar`**: rendered **below** the main body, **outside** the `ScrollArea`, in a `shrink-0` strip with `border-t`, `bg-background`, and horizontal padding aligned with body (`px-6 pr-1` to mirror `contentClassName`’s `pr-1`).
- Scroll layout is now a **column**: `ScrollArea` is `min-h-0 flex-1` so the list uses **CoverView’s scroll only**; `bottomBar` stays pinned to the bottom of the panel.
- The same **`bottomBar`** strip is rendered in the **`scrollable={false}`** branch so behavior stays consistent.

### 2. `DetailPanelShell` (`detail-panel-chrome.tsx`)
- New optional **`bottomBar`** prop, passed through to **`CoverView`**.

### 3. `ProfileEditPanel` (`profile-edit-panel.tsx`)
- Removed the local **`ScrollArea`** and any **sticky** remainder.
- Passes **`ScoringRemainder`** as **`bottomBar`** on **`DetailPanelShell`** so it stays fixed under the scroll area while actions, copy, category blocks, and “Open full config” scroll inside **CoverView**.

No nested scroll: one scroll (CoverView viewport), remainder docked on the panel bottom via layout, not `position: fixed` to the window (so it stays correct inside the stacked panel).

[REDACTED]

 But base the mean score and max score on the current mean and max.  so it can be dynamically increased. 
</user_query>

content.text:
<user_query>
I think you may have misunderstood what I meant. I'll rephrase.

Currently, the discrete progress bar shows a visualization for each score compared to the available minimum and maximum values. The maximum value for each score should be based on the current maximum score across all scores. This means the score that is currently the largest will show as 100% progress.

As soon as another score increases beyond that previous highest score, the previous highest score will no longer be shown as 100% progress.
</user_query>

Reviewing the implementation and aligning the progress bar so each row uses scale 0 → global current maximum (leader = 100%).

[REDACTED]

Aligning behavior: progress is always value ÷ current global maximum (minimum 0) so the leader shows 100%. Fixing the profile panel, which still used `MARBLE_TARGET` as the bar cap. Removing unused `progressMin` / `resolveScoringProgressExtent` complexity.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was wrong and what we changed.

### Intended behavior
- Progress is **value ÷ current global maximum** (implicit **minimum 0**).
- **Every row** uses the **same** `max`, equal to the **largest score right now** (or the configured ceiling when all scores are 0), so the leader is always **100%** and drops as soon as someone scores higher.

That already matches `ScoringComplete` (`progressMax = highest > 0 ? highest : settings.maxScore`). The math in `ScoringDiscreteProgress` with `min === 0` is exactly `position = value`, `range = max`, so no change was required there beyond **docs** and **aria-label**.

### What was wrong
- **`profile-edit-panel`** passed `max={MARBLE_TARGET}` (117) for every row, so bars were always scaled to the full marble budget, **not** to the current top score.

### What we did
1. **`profile-edit-panel.tsx`** — `progressCap = max(marble weights)`, using `MARBLE_TARGET` only when every weight is `0` (same idea as `maxScore` in the story). Each `ScoringListItem` now gets `max={progressCap}`.
2. **`ScoringComplete.tsx`** — Removed unused `resolveScoringProgressExtent` import and trimmed `aggregateScores` to only `pointsLeft` and `highest`. Clarified the JSDoc that the bar is **0 … current highest** (or `maxScore` while all scores are 0).
3. **Removed** `scoring-progress-extent.ts` and the **`progressMin`** API on `ScoringListItem` / `ScoreItem` (always 0 for this model).
4. **`ScoringDiscreteProgress`** — Documented that **`max`** is the shared cap (current global max). **`min`** stays optional for other cases (e.g. remainder-style ranges); list rows don’t pass it.

So: the leader stays at full bar; raising any score above the old max lowers everyone else’s **relative** fill, including the previous leader.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
The number of marbles to distribute should be directly related to the number of criteria to score.

For example, with 5 available marbles per criterion, when all marbles are distributed each criterion should score an average of 5.
</user_query>

Tracing how `MARBLE_TARGET` and criterion counts are used so we can tie the marble pool to `criteria.length`.

[REDACTED]

Implementing a dynamic marble pool: `criteria.length × MARBLE_AVG_PER_CRITERION` (default 5), and updating normalization and UI.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Biome check on modified frontend files

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented.

### Rule
- **Pool total** = `criteria.length × MARBLE_AVG_PER_CRITERION` (default **5**).
- So with **N** criteria, **N×5** marbles are shared; if every marble is assigned, the **average weight per criterion is 5**.

### `apps/frontend-web/src/lib/profile-marbles.ts`
- Replaced fixed **`MARBLE_TARGET` (117)** with:
  - **`MARBLE_AVG_PER_CRITERION = 5`**
  - **`profileMarblePoolTotal(criterionCount)`** → `max(0, n) * 5`
- **`normalizeAllProfileWeightsToMarbleTarget`** now normalizes each profile to **`profileMarblePoolTotal(config.criteria.length)`**, not 117.

### `profile-edit-panel.tsx`
- Uses **`marblePool`** from `profileMarblePoolTotal(criterionIds.length)` for: load normalization, save merge, remainder `totalPoints`, `pointsLeft`, per-row `canIncrement` cap (`value < marblePool`), and progress cap fallback.
- Copy explains **5 × criterion count** and that a full pool implies **average 5 per criterion**.

### `profile-detail-panel.tsx`
- Footnote uses the same **`marblePool`** and **`MARBLE_AVG_PER_CRITERION`** wording.

After the next save, every profile’s weights sum to **5 × (number of criteria)** instead of 117; existing configs rescale on that path. To change the target average, adjust **`MARBLE_AVG_PER_CRITERION`** only.

[REDACTED]

</user_query>

content.text:
Fixing the TypeScript error in `ScoringDiscreteProgress` (custom CSS variable not in `CSSProperties`) and adding a brief JSDoc on `Segment.colorClass`.

[REDACTED]

Here is what was finished to wire **theme category colors** through the app and scoring UI.

### Theme and helpers
- **`category-scoring-color.ts`** — Maps `CAT_LOC` … `CAT_PRA` to `bg-category-*`, `border-l-category-*`, and `var(--color-category-*)` for fills; unknown ids still use chart fallbacks. Removed the unused `CategoryId` import earlier; JSDoc uses plain text.

### Exports
- **`presentation/index.ts`** — Re-exports `categoryScoringBarClass`, `categoryScoringFillVarRef`, and `categoryScoringLeadBorderClass`.

### Discrete / list scoring
- **`ScoringDiscreteProgress`** — Optional `categoryId` sets `--scoring-category-fill` on a wrapper; discrete segments and the linear `Progress` indicator use `bg-[color:var(--scoring-category-fill)]`. Custom property typing is fixed with an `as CSSProperties` cast.
- **`ScoreItem` / `ScoringListItem`** — Pass `categoryId` through to `ScoringDiscreteProgress`.
- **`ScoringComplete`** — Optional `categoryId` on each item; stories use rotating category ids.

### App and stories
- **`profile-detail-panel.tsx`** & **`plot-profiles-dashboard-page.tsx`** — `categoryWeightSegments` now uses `categoryScoringBarClass(categoryId)` instead of a fixed color array (dashboard widget + profile detail bars match categories).
- **`profile-edit-panel.tsx`** — Category headers use `border-l-4` + `categoryScoringLeadBorderClass(group.categoryId)`; each `ScoringListItem` gets `categoryId={group.categoryId}`.
- **Fixtures / Storybook** — `plot-profile-dashboard-widget.fixtures.tsx`, `presentation-blocks.stories.tsx`, and `Scoring.stories.tsx` use real `CAT_*` ids with `categoryScoringBarClass` / demo `categoryId`s.

### Docs
- **`segmented-progress.tsx`** — JSDoc on `Segment.colorClass` points callers at `categoryScoringBarClass`.

`vite build` for `frontend-web` succeeds. `packages/ui-lib`’s full `tsc` still reports many **existing** issues (e.g. `Card` `variant`, `iconStroke`); the only change from this work there was the **`ScoringDiscreteProgress`** style typing fix.

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### `profile-edit-panel.tsx`
- Import **`useConfirm`** from `@portugal/ui` (same imperative API as under `AppProvider`; your **`ManagementAppShell`** already wraps the tree with **`AlertDialogProvider`**, which supplies this hook).
- **`Reset to average (5)`** button next to Cancel / Save (disabled if `confirm` is missing or there are no criteria).
- **`handleResetToAverage`**: `await confirm(title, message)`; on **Confirm**, sets **every** criterion draft weight to **`MARBLE_AVG_PER_CRITERION`** (5), so the draft matches the documented target (full pool ⇒ mean 5 per criterion). Nothing is persisted until **Save & normalize marbles**.

Copy in the dialog states that the draft is replaced until save or leaving the panel.

[REDACTED]

@portugal/packages/ui/src/styles/themes/obsidian.css @portugal/packages/ui/src/styles/themes/ocean.css  I also added these colors to the Obsidian and Ocean themes.  use vibrant colors. 
</user_query>

Reading the three theme files to align category colors and make Obsidian and Ocean more vibrant.

[REDACTED]

Adding `--category-cat-*` overrides to Obsidian (dark + light) and Ocean (dark + light) with higher-chroma oklch values so category bars read clearly on each theme’s surfaces.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
--chart-5: oklch(0.5 0.2 290);

  /* Light: still expose obs-* for components using bg-surface etc. */

content.text:
Here’s what changed.

### Obsidian (`obsidian.css`)
- **`html[data-theme="obsidian"].dark`** — Five scoring categories use **high-chroma** oklch colors (chroma mostly **0.18–0.26**, lightness **0.72–0.84**) so bars read as clear neon accents on the dark obsidian stack (electric blue, mint, chartreuse, magenta, warm orange).
- **`html[data-theme="obsidian"]:not(.dark)`** — Same hue roles with **strong saturation** on light UI (chroma **0.2–0.26**, lightness **~0.46–0.52**) so segments stay vivid on lavender-white surfaces.

### Ocean (`ocean.css`)
- **`html[data-theme="ocean"]:not(.dark)`** — **Coastal** palette: azure, sea-green, lime-gold, violet, amber with chroma **0.18–0.22** on white cards.
- **`html[data-theme="ocean"].dark`** — **Brighter** fills on deep blue-grey (L **0.76–0.85**, chroma **0.14–0.18**) so category bars pop without matching the muted chart stack.

`globals.css` already wires `--color-category-*` → `var(--category-cat-*)`, so `categoryScoringBarClass` / fills pick these up automatically when `data-theme` is `obsidian` or `ocean`.

[REDACTED]

Simplifying the reset button: `confirm` is always available under root `AppProvider`.

content.text:
Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
`ManagementAppShell` wrapped only the **main column** in `AlertDialogProvider`. In `Panels.tsx`, **dock/overlay panels are siblings** of that column (`fixed` panel strip), so `ProfileEditPanel` never sat under that provider and `useConfirm()` was always `undefined`.

### Changes
1. **`apps/frontend-web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrap the app with **`AppProvider`** (inside `AuthProvider`, around `AppLayout`) so `AlertDialogProvider` + `useConfirm` / `useApp` cover **main + panels**.

2. **`packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/management-app-shell.tsx`** — Remove the inner **`AlertDialogProvider`** so you don’t nest two dialog trees; dialogs are supplied once at the root.

3. **`packages/ui/src/components/sewdn/AppShell/AppProvider.tsx`** — Document that **`AppProvider` must sit outside** shell + `PanelsLayout` so side panels get the same confirm context.

4. **`packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/app-shell.stories.tsx`** — Wrap the **Authenticated** story with **`AppProvider`** so Storybook matches the real app.

5. **`profile-edit-panel.tsx`** — Reset is only **`disabled={criterionIds.length === 0}`**; `handleResetToAverage` still bails if `!confirm` for safety.

You can keep using **`useConfirm()`** from `@portugal/ui`, or **`useApp().confirm?.confirm(...)`** if you prefer the app context API.

[REDACTED]
